content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed `origin/master..HEAD`, read the prompt-contract/generator/stage/template changes end-to-end, and ran targeted prompt-contract rendering tests. I found two real issues: the new contract-shape validator is now too permissive to enforce canonical heading order reliably, and the commit also removes the tracked repo workspace config, which breaks clean-checkout repo-root commands.

## Amendments

1. `validate_canonical_prompt_shape()` no longer guarantees that the canonical headings are unique and truly in order. In `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:131-144`, it now scans forward from the previous match and accepts the first later occurrence of each heading, so a malformed prompt with an earlier stray `## Acceptance Criteria` (or any other canonical heading) still passes as long as the heading appears again later in the expected slot. The new test at `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:218-225` explicitly codifies that permissive behavior. Because `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:245-253` uses this function as the final gate before accepting a refined prompt, prompt review can now persist contract-drifted prompts that still violate the promised canonical section order.
   Rationale: This weakens the main safety check for the new prompt contract: downstream stages can receive a prompt whose canonical headings are duplicated or semantically out of order even though prompt review says the contract was preserved.
2. The diff deletes the checked-in repo workspace config `.ralph-burning/workspace.toml` (whole file removed), but repo-root commands still unconditionally require that file via `src/contexts/workspace_governance/mod.rs:65-66`. In a clean export of `HEAD`, running `ralph-burning config show` from the repo root now fails with `No such file or directory`, while the same command succeeds from `origin/master`. Unless this repo is intentionally no longer supposed to be an initialized workspace, removing the tracked workspace scaffold is a regression unrelated to the prompt-contract feature.
   Rationale: Merging this leaves the branch dependent on untracked local files for basic repo-root workflows and breaks clean-checkout behavior.
